name: shared-core
description: Language-agnostic orchestration, meta-skills, governance. Any language adapter depends on this.

aspirational_guardrails.G81: Baselines-not-lowered enforcement. Design rule decided in v0.2.0; script not yet authored.
aspirational_guardrails.G83: Per-run decision-log entry caps. Caps documented in CLAUDE.md rule 11; script not yet authored.
aspirational_guardrails.G84: Output-shape hash compensating baseline. Decided in CLAUDE.md rule 28 v0.3.0; script not yet authored.

notes.v0_6_migration_status: stub. Arrays grow per migration batch as files arrive from v0.5.0. validate-packages.sh passes against the empty manifest because the corresponding fs locations are also empty.
notes.waves_authoritative_source: Wave-id values mirror the canonical wave names in phases/{DESIGN,IMPLEMENTATION,TESTING,FEEDBACK}-PHASE.md. Each shared-core agent appears in exactly the wave its phase doc declares. Per-language packs (go.json / python.json) contribute additional agents to the same wave-ids; the impl/design/testing/feedback leads union across all active packages at runtime. Mode-conditional waves use the `_mode_bc` / `_mode_a` suffix per the convention documented in sdk-design-lead.md and sdk-impl-lead.md.
notes.agents_not_in_waves: Seven agents intentionally omitted from `waves` because they are NOT dispatched by a phase lead via WAVE_AGENTS resolution: (1) the four orchestrators sdk-{intake,design,impl,testing}-lead are themselves the wave dispatchers; (2) sdk-marker-scanner runs at start of Mode B/C analyze step BEFORE any phase lead spawns and is invoked by the pipeline driver, not by a wave; (3) sdk-drift-detector runs as a long-lived background observer over the T-SOAK period and is launched directly by the testing lead's soak orchestration prose, not via a discrete wave; (4) defect-analyzer is consumed inside F3_root_cause by root-cause-tracer rather than spawned independently. These intentional omissions are not dispatch gaps.
